key_insight: A 5D Polycrystal Yield Surface interpolation table lets FEM solvers approximate VPSC-based irradiation creep and growth in zirconium alloys with much lower computational cost.
engineering_relevance: This makes multi-scale simulation of nuclear core components such as spacer grids and fuel cladding more practical for design, lifetime assessment, and safety-margin studies.
method: Embed a static 5D interpolation table in Code_Aster to represent instantaneous viscoplastic response.
Use periodic recalibration plus first-order Taylor linearization to follow microstructural drift from radiation damage.
Benchmark the surrogate against full VPSC-FEM simulations under continuous high-dose irradiation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the linearization scheme keeps relative errors below 1% on representative deformation paths and that spacer-grid simulations achieve meaningful computational savings while preserving fidelity.
watch_item: The approach still relies on periodic recalibration, so its accuracy and robustness depend on how well the chosen update schedule covers long-term irradiation-induced state changes.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
